title: EndpointType::from_uri causes double allocation on unknown URI path
description: crates/jmap-chat/src/types.rs lines 175-199 — EndpointType::deserialize allocates a String via String::deserialize(d), then passes it as &str to from_uri(), which on the unrecognized-URI path calls other.to_string() (line 184) — a second heap allocation for the same data. The five new enums (ChatMemberRole, ChatKind, BodyType, OwnerPresence, PushUrgency) all avoid this by moving the already-owned String directly into Unknown(raw). The fix is to inline the match in deserialize and do Unknown(s) rather than routing through from_uri(&s). The from_uri(&str) public API can be kept as-is for callers who have a &str, but deserialize should not use it. Impact is one extra String clone per deserialized unknown EndpointType value — minor in practice but avoidable.
